LAW AND MOTION, 301, PLAINTIFF JOHN DOE'S MOTION FOR CLARIFICATION WHETHER THE SAN FRANCISCO DEFTS WERE DISMISSED AND TO VACATE DISMISSAL OF THE MASSACHUSETTS DEFTS. THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR CLARIFICATION WHETHER THE SAN FRANCISCO DEFENDANTS WERE DISMISSED AND TO VACATE DISMISSAL OF THE MASSACHUSETTS DEFENDANTS, MOTION TO VACATE IS DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SATISY C C P SECTION 1008 WITH RESPECT TO RECONSIDERATION OF THE JUNE 4, 2007 ORDER WHICH DISMISSED DEFENDANTS CAMBRIDGE HOUSING AUTHORITY AND MICHAEL JOHNSTON. LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - DEFENDANT MICHAEL JOHNSTON, CAMBRIDGE HOUSING AUTHORITY'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE IS GRANTED. THE EARLIER DETERMINATION IN CASE 438600 THAT DEFENDANTS WERE NOT SUBJECT TO CALIFORNIA JURISDICTION BARS RELITIGATION OF THAT ISSUE IN THIS CASE. ANY REQUEST BY DEFENDANTS FOR FEES IN CONNECTION WITH PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO RECONSIDER WAS MERELY A STATUTORILY PERMITTED INCIDENT OF DEFFENDANTS LITIGATION OF THEIR POSITION THAT THE COURT LACKED JURISDICTION AND SOUGHT ONLY WHAT THE COURT MIGHT GRANT UNDER CCP 1008(D) CONSISTENT WITH THAT POSITION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: WENDY PUN, CSR # 12891

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY, TONY UCCIFERRI, JOY CONDE, AND SANDRA CAMPBELL'S MOTION TO DISMISS PLAINTIFF JOHN DOE'S COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THIS CASE WAS FILED TO RE-LITIGATE CASE # 438600, AND TO FRUSTRATE OR ABUSE PROCESS AGAINST THE DEFENDANTS. THIS COMPLAINT IS A SHAM PLEADING, AND IS DISMISSED. SEE NEAL VS. BANK OF AMERICA (1949) 93 CAL.APP. 2ND 678. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR 5759

Sfha, Et Al Motion To Dismiss
Off Calendar May-26-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Defendants San Francisco Housing Authority, Tony Ucciferri, Joy Conde, and Sandra Campbell's Motion to Dismiss Plaintiff John Doe's Complaint. Appearances: Colin H. Jewell, Esq. of Walsworth Franklin Bevins & McCall LLP (415) 781-7072, appearing for Defendants and moving party; John Doe, Jr., Plaintiff in Pro Per appearing and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is Granted. This case was filed to re-litigate Case # 438600, and to frustrate or abuse process against the defendants. This complaint is a sham pleading, and is dismissed. See Neal vs. Bank of America (1949) 93 Cal.App. 2nd 678. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW/AY)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service
Off Calendar Apr-17-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES- JOHN DOE, TEL. NO. (711)761-0259 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. RANDALL J. LEE, TEL. NO. 781-7072 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - DEFENDANT MICHAEL JOHNSTON, CAMBRIDGE HOUSING AUTHORITY'S Motion To Quash Service IS GRANTED. THE EARLIER DETERMINATION IN CASE 438600 THAT DEFENDANTS WERE NOT SUBJECT TO CALIFORNIA JURISDICTION BARS RELITIGATION OF THAT ISSUE IN THIS CASE. ANY REQUEST BY DEFENDANTS FOR FEES IN CONNECTION WITH PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO RECONSIDER WAS MERELY A STATUTORILY PERMITTED INCIDENT OF DEFFENDANTS LITIGATION OF THEIR POSITION THAT THE COURT LACKED JURISDICTION AND SOUGHT ONLY WHAT THE COURT MIGHT GRANT UNDER CCP 1008(d) CONSISTENT WITH THAT POSITION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Wendy Pun, CSR # 12891 =(301/PJB/JF)
